ApplyTypes.brief: Constants defining attribute types, effect locations, and special effect identifiers within the game's affect system.
ApplyTypes.details: The ApplyTypes group encompasses a comprehensive set of constants used to categorize and identify various attributes, effects, and effect locations in the game's affect system. These constants include core character attributes such as strength, dexterity, intelligence, wisdom, constitution, and charisma, as well as combat-related attributes like hit points, stamina, armor class, hitroll, and damroll. Additionally, they cover effects related to resistances, saving throws, experience and skill points, resource costs (mana and stamina), damage percentages, and special effect locations for unique or class-specific effects. Many constants serve as identifiers for effect slots or bonus vectors, enabling consistent application, modification, and display of effects across different game mechanics. The inclusion of dedicated constants for unique effects, such as forge, priestess, brewing, scribing, tanking, necromancer effects, and character-specific auras, supports flexible customization and extension of the affect system, ensuring organized and maintainable effect handling throughout the codebase.
ApplyTypes.notes: Constants are organized to distinguish between core attributes, combat stats, resource effects, resistances, and special effect locations. This organization facilitates modular effect application and simplifies effect management, especially when adding new effects or customizing existing ones for different classes or items.
ApplyTypes.rationale: The ApplyTypes group exists to provide a standardized and maintainable way to reference various effect and attribute types within the game's affect system. By centralizing these identifiers, the system ensures consistent effect handling, simplifies effect application logic, and supports future extensions for new effects or attributes, thereby enhancing code clarity and reducing errors in effect management.
ApplyTypes.name: ApplyTypes

APPLY_NONE.brief: A constant indicating that no specific attribute or effect location is targeted.
APPLY_NONE.details: APPLY_NONE is a sentinel constant used to signify that an effect or attribute modification does not apply to any particular attribute or location. Initialized to zero, it is frequently used in effect-handling functions such as spells and affect modifications to denote effects that are not attribute-specific, such as invisibility or general protections. This constant provides a clear default or placeholder value, enabling the code to distinguish between effects that target specific locations and those that do not, thereby facilitating effect management and logic branching.
APPLY_NONE.notes: Typically stored as zero, this constant is used as a default value in effect and attribute processing routines to indicate the absence of a specific target location.
APPLY_NONE.rationale: The constant exists to support effects that do not modify specific attributes, simplifying effect handling logic by providing a standard indicator for 'no location' scenarios.
APPLY_NONE.name: APPLY_NONE
